title: ACP child sessions do not inherit subagent security envelope constraints in spawnAcpDirect
description: In spawnAcpDirect, an ACP child session is created from a requesting (possibly restricted subagent) session without resolving or enforcing the subagent security envelope. The requester state is resolved without a subagent capability store, no maximum-depth or active-child-count check is performed, and the gateway sessions.patch that creates the child session persists only spawnedBy and label, omitting spawnDepth, subagentRole, and subagentControlScope. As a result a restricted subagent can spawn an ACP child that escapes subagent-only constraints such as depth, child-count limits, control scope, and target-agent restrictions (CWE-277, insecure inherited permissions).
